description:
## Situation

The HELiX admin dashboard (apps/admin, Next.js 15, port 3159) currently has a sophisticated 17-dimension health scoring system that monitors the local hx-library web component library. Phase 1 (complete) bridged this with the wc-tools MCP server — admin scores now sync outbound to wc-tools history files, and MCP data (branch diffs, CEM a11y profiles) flows inbound to the admin UI.

The wc-tools MCP server (separate repo) already supports ANY web component library via its CEM-based tooling. It has CDN CEM resolution (resolve_cdn_cem), framework detection, and 31+ tools across 11 categories. But the admin dashboard is hardcoded to a single local library.

## Problem

1. **Single-library lock-in**: The admin dashboard only monitors hx-library. Enterprise teams managing multiple WC libraries (or evaluating third-party options) have no unified view.
2. **No CDN scoring**: wc-tools can fetch CEM from npm/CDN for any published library (Shoelace, Spectrum Web Components, Carbon, Lion, etc.) but the admin has no UI to leverage this.
3. **No cross-library comparison**: Teams can't benchmark their library against industry leaders.
4. **wc-tools history gaps**: The MCP server's history format doesn't support the admin's 17-dimension breakdown with confidence levels. get_health_trend returns basic scores, not per-dimension trends.
5. **No library registry**: No way to add/remove/configure monitored libraries through the UI.

## Approach

Transform the admin dashboard into a universal WC management console with multi-library support.

### Architecture

**Library Registry** (new concept):
- JSON config at apps/admin/src/data/library-registry.json
- Each entry: { id, name, source: "local" | "cdn" | "npm", cemPath | packageName, prefix, mcpConfig }
- Default entry: hx-library (local, current behavior)
- CRUD UI at /libraries route

**Multi-Library MCP Client**:
- Extend mcp-client.ts to support multiple CEM sources
- For CDN libraries: spawn wc-tools with MCP_WC_CEM_PATH pointing to cached CDN CEM
- For local libraries: use existing spawn pattern
- Session pool: one McpSession per library, shared 5-min cache

**Scoring Engine Adaptation**:
- Local libraries: full 17-dimension scoring (source code access)
- CDN libraries: CEM-only scoring (6 dimensions from wc-tools) + whatever wc-tools can derive
- Display both with clear "CEM-only" vs "full analysis" indicators

**wc-tools Enhancements** (separate repo PRs):
- Accept richer history format with weights and confidence in HistoryFileSchema
- New get_health_summary tool: aggregate stats for all components in one call
- Expose confidence levels in score_component when history has admin-written data
- Per-dimension trend data in get_health_trend (not just overall score)

### New Routes

- /libraries — Library registry CRUD, add via npm package name or local path
- /libraries/[id] — Single library dashboard (existing /health view but parameterized)
- /compare — Side-by-side comparison of 2-4 libraries
- /benchmark — Your library vs industry benchmarks (pre-configured CDN libraries)

### Key Types

```typescript
interface LibraryEntry {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  source: 'local' | 'cdn' | 'npm';
  cemPath?: string;        // For local
  packageName?: string;    // For CDN/npm
  prefix: string;          // e.g., "hx-", "sl-", "md-"
  lastScored?: string;     // ISO date
  componentCount?: number;
}
```

## Results

1. **Multi-library monitoring**: Register any WC library (local or published) and see health scores
2. **Industry benchmarking**: Compare your library against Shoelace, Spectrum, Carbon, etc.
3. **Unified dashboard**: One console for all your WC libraries
4. **Richer MCP data**: wc-tools returns 17-dimension trends with confidence levels
5. **CDN scoring**: Score any npm-published WC library without cloning it

## Constraints

- Admin scorer remains source of truth for local libraries. CDN libraries get CEM-only scores — never claim full 17-dim for remote libraries.
- wc-tools enhancements are separate repo PRs (different project board).
- Graceful degradation: everything must work when wc-tools is unavailable, and CDN libraries must work when the library isn't installed locally.
- No breaking changes to existing admin routes or health-scorer.ts.
- CDN CEM caching must respect npm registry rate limits.
- Library registry stored as JSON file, not database (admin is a dev tool, not SaaS).
title: Phase 2: Universal Web Component Management & Health Console
